title: Cyclistic Chicago Cleaned Trip Data

resources.description: Cleaned ride-level Divvy data (Jun 2025 – May 2026), gzip-compressed. Notebook 04 decompresses it automatically.

fields.name: rideable_type
fields.description: Bike type used: classic_bike or electric_bike.

fields.name: start_station_name
fields.description: Name of the station where the trip started.

fields.name: end_station_name
fields.description: Name of the station where the trip ended.

fields.name: member_casual
fields.description: Rider type: 'member' (annual subscriber) or 'casual' (single-ride / day pass).

subtitle: 5.8M cleaned Divvy bike-share trip records (Chicago, Jun 2025-May 2026)
description:
Hey there! 👋

This is the clean, analysis-ready version of Chicago's Divvy bike-share data — the official trip records Cyclistic riders generate every day, with all the messy bits already scrubbed out so you can get straight to the analysis. 🚴‍♂️

The raw Divvy data is great, but let's be honest — it's messy. Broken or out-of-order timestamps, rides that last 3 seconds (or 3 days 😅), missing station info, duplicate IDs, impossible durations... I took 12 months of rides (Jun 2025 → May 2026, ~5.8M trips) and cleaned all of it.

## What I did 🧼

- Removed duplicate ride IDs
- Dropped rides with broken, negative or absurd durations
- Handled missing station names, IDs and coordinates
- Standardized data types (proper datetimes, numeric coordinates)
- Kept the original Divvy schema intact — no engineered features, no opinions, just clean source data you can build on

## What's inside 📋

The standard Divvy trip schema (13 columns), one row per ride: ride id, bike type, start/end timestamps, start/end station names & IDs, start/end coordinates, and rider type (member vs casual).

## What can you use it for? 📊

- **Member vs casual analysis:** the classic Cyclistic case study (split here is ~64% member / 36% casual).
- **Build your own features:** trip duration, day-of-week, holidays, spatial joins — all yours to engineer from a clean base.
- **Spatial analysis & maps:** start/end coordinates are clean and ready to plot. 🗺️
- **Temporal patterns:** rush-hour peaks, weekend waves, seasonal trends.

Perfect for data analysts, urban geeks, bike-sharing researchers, or anyone doing the Google Data Analytics capstone who wants a clean dataset to start from! 🚴‍♂️🏙️

Grab it, group by member_casual, and start exploring. 🌍✨
